title: HTTP endpoints: unbounded POST body accumulation enables local DoS
author: chirag127
body:
## Problem
`parseParams` concatenates every POST chunk into a single in-memory string with no size cap; a large or slow-stream POST to any `/api/*` endpoint pins memory until OOM.

## Steps
1. Fresh install witsy 3.6.0 (default `enableHttpEndpoints: true`, default port 8090).
2. From any local process or browser tab:
   ```
   curl -X POST -H 'Content-Type: application/json' --data-binary @/dev/zero http://localhost:8090/api/chat
   ```

## Expected
Request rejected with 413 once body exceeds a sane cap (e.g. 1 MB).

## Actual
`data += chunk` at `src/main/http_utils.ts:34-39` grows unbounded; renderer process memory climbs until Electron main crashes.

## Cite
- `src/main/http_utils.ts:34-39` (no length check)
- `src/main/http_server.ts:83-84` (bound to 127.0.0.1, but any local process/browser tab can reach it)
- `defaults/settings.json` (`enableHttpEndpoints: true`)
- `src/consts.ts:3` (`kDefaultHttpPort = 8090`)

## Environment
witsy 3.6.0, Node 22 (per `@tsconfig/node22`), Electron 38.2.0.

Thanks for maintaining Kochava-Studios/witsy!

title: The prompt enhancement button ("magic wand") always replaces the input with a hardcoded SaaS background prompt
author: A-X3L
body: Hi team, First of all, thank you for this amazing tool! I am loving the local workflow with Ollama and Forge. I think I found a small development fallback/mock that accidentally made its way into the production build. When clicking on the magic wand button (Prompt Enhancer) in the Design Studio, instead of dynamically enhancing the prompt, it always completely replaces the user's input with the exact same static text: "A dynamic, colorful abstract background designed for a modern SaaS website. The image features flowing gradients with vibrant hues of blue, purple, pink..." Steps to reproduce: Open the Design Studio. Type any prompt. Click the magic wand icon. The prompt is instantly overwritten by the SaaS background description. Technical hint: I dug a little bit into the compiled JavaScript files via VS Code and found that this specific string is hardcoded right inside the @vue/reactivity and core rendering blocks. It seems the function is currently returning this static string instead of triggering the actual LLM/Ollama enhancement API call. Thanks for your help and keep up the great work! 🙏 Edit: Added a screenshot from VS Code. It looks like the prompt is defined as a static default value inside the image-create token presets array (const aFi), which bypasses the user's actual input when the action is triggered.

title: Proposition d’amélioration — Séparer rôle/style/optimisations du modèle (architecture plus modulaire et plus fiable)
author: Z1t0on
body:
Bonjour l’équipe Witsy 👋

En utilisant Witsy avec Ollama, j’ai remarqué un comportement qui limite la modularité et la fiabilité des réponses lorsque l’on combine :

modèles

modelfiles

styles

experts

paramètres avancés

Aujourd’hui, Witsy associe les optimisations matérielles (num_ctx, température, top_p, etc.) au modèle, ce qui revient à figer un rôle/persona/optimisation dans le modèle lui‑même.
Cela entraîne plusieurs effets secondaires :

multiplication des modelfiles pour un même modèle (ex : 3 rôles → 3 modelfiles → 4 entrées dans ollama list)

rigidité : chaque modelfile devient un “modèle figé” dans un rôle

pollution de la liste Ollama

experts moins portables

collisions de paramètres lorsque Witsy re‑prompte

comportements imprévisibles selon l’ordre des couches

difficulté à changer de quantization (Q4 → Q5 → Q8) sans tout recréer

🎯 Proposition : séparer clairement les couches
J’ai expérimenté une approche différente, très simple à mettre en œuvre, qui consiste à :

garder le modèle nu (aucun rôle, aucun style, aucune optimisation dedans)

mettre le rôle, le style et les paramètres spécifiques dans l’expert

appliquer les paramètres matériels via une couche indépendante

laisser Witsy empiler les couches comme prévu (expert → style → instruction)

ne jamais figer un rôle dans un modelfile

Résultat :

un modèle = un “cerveau” neutre

un expert = un rôle complet (persona + style + optimisations)

les paramètres matériels sont appliqués dynamiquement

les experts deviennent universels et greffables sur n’importe quel modèle

la liste Ollama reste propre (4 entrées, RAG inclus)

changer de quantization = un simple ollama pull

aucune rigidité, aucune duplication

comportement beaucoup plus stable et prévisible

🔧 Prototype fonctionnel : Interceptor.py (5 ko)
Pour illustrer cette approche, j’ai codé un petit proxy ultra simple (Interceptor.py) qui met en pratique exactement la logique décrite ci‑dessus.

Il :

empêche les modelfiles/styles d’injecter des paramètres matériels

protège la VRAM

rend les experts totalement portables

laisse Witsy faire sa fusion normalement

Je joins le fichier directement dans cette Issue (5 ko).
Il est autonome, lisible, et montre clairement la séparation des couches que je propose.

Merci pour votre travail, Witsy est un super projet ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ et je pense que cette séparation des couches pourrait le rendre encore plus robuste et modulaire.

[Interceptor.py](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29322436/Interceptor.py)

title: Bug: Sidebar links overflow and hide the settings icon on 1080p screens with 125% Windows scaling
author: Z1t0on
body: **Describe the bug** When adding multiple web applications to the sidebar, the layout breaks on 1080p screens with a 125% Windows scaling factor. The sidebar lacks an internal scrollbar (`overflow-y: auto`), which causes the bottom links—specifically the settings gear icon—to overflow and be completely hidden underneath the bottom application version banner (`Witsy v3.5.2`). **To Reproduce** Steps to reproduce the behavior: 1. Go to 'Settings' > 'Sidebar'. 2. Enable 6 or 7 web applications (e.g., Google Drive, Notebook LM, Photos, Translation, Gemini, Canva, Gamma). 3. Look at the left main sidebar. 4. See error: The settings gear icon at the very bottom disappears behind the bottom status bar unless you disable some web apps or force a 90% zoom in the app. **Expected behavior** The sidebar should either have an internal scrollbar or the icons should adapt so that critical UI items like the settings wheel always remain visible. **Desktop:** - OS: Windows 11 / 10 - Resolution: 1920x1080 with 125% Display Scaling - Witsy Version: v3.5.2

title: My agent created in Witsy can't "see" the content
author: victorxstc
body:
**Describe the bug**
My agent in Witsy reads neither the attached file, nor the pasted text (which are attached and pasted into Witsy's own chat box). Note that the API of LM Studio 0.4.16 works perfectly in normal chat from within Witsy. But in agentic mode, I have difficulty make the agent become aware of the content.

**To Reproduce**

1. Open Witsy and link it to LM Studio API
2. Make sure the LM Studio API works (by simple-chatting in Witsy)
3. Create an agent with the instructions to open and read and summarize the attached document (PDF or DOCX) and/or to read the pasted text.
4. Create a new chat in Witsy
5. Attach a document to the Witsy's chatbox and also paste text in the chat box of Witsy.
6. Run the agent


**Expected behavior**
I expect that the agent can "see" the document that is attached to the Witsy's chat box and also the text pasted within Witsy's text box. 

**What actually happens?**
Nevertheless, model seems to be blind to the Witsy's environment (the attached document within Witsy, or the text pasted in Witsy's chat window). The model says the following:

> Okay, so the user asked me to summarize something, but they didn't provide any text or file. Hmm, that makes it tricky because without content, I can't really do much. I remember earlier when they tried using some tool, there was an error about a 404. Maybe that's why they're asking again....

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Windows 10 x64
 - Witsy Version: 3.5.2
 - LM Studio: 0.4.16 -- Working perfectly fine with Witsy, in normal chat mode.

**Additional context**
Just please note that the API of LM Studio works just perfectly fine. The problem is that I can't make the agent "see" the Witsy's chat window or the document attached in the chat window of Witsy.

title: fr locale missing 5 sections from en -- happy to keep it in sync
author: isabellehuecloser-ctrl
body: Witsy's fr.json is a great start but is missing 5 sections that en.json added recently -- looks like computerUse, deepResearch, designStudio, dictation, and drawingCanvas -- so French users hit raw English strings in those newer AI flows. A GitHub Action called i18n Autopilot (https://github.com/marketplace/actions/i18n-autopilot) auto-fills new sections into fr.json and opens a PR whenever en.json changes, so fr never drifts behind again. Happy to open that catch-up PR myself to fill in those 5 sections -- no worries either way 🙌

Please could it be added?

title: MCP OAuth DCR fails for public-client-only servers (Fastmail): token_endpoint_auth_method must be "none"
author: GravitationChen
body: # MCP OAuth DCR fails for public-client-only servers (Fastmail): `token_endpoint_auth_method must be "none"` ## Summary Adding a remote MCP server that enforces **OAuth 2.1 public-client registration** (PKCE-only, no client secret) fails during Dynamic Client Registration (RFC 7591). Witsy's DCR request appears to omit `token_endpoint_auth_method` or send a value other than `"none"`, which causes strict OAuth servers — including Fastmail's official MCP — to reject the registration outright. This blocks integration with a growing list of major remote MCP servers that require public-client DCR. ## Error Message ``` Authentication setup failed Error invoking remote method 'mcp-start-oauth-flow': nd: invalid_client_metadata token_endpoint_auth_method must be "none" ``` (The leading `nd:` in the message also looks like a truncation/formatting bug — likely the tail end of `…/oauth/register responded:` or similar. Worth checking the error formatter too.) ## Steps to Reproduce 1. Open Witsy → **Settings → MCP / Plugins → Add Server**. 2. Add a new remote MCP server with URL: `https://api.fastmail.com/mcp` 3. Trigger the OAuth flow (e.g., "Authenticate" / "Connect"). 4. Observe the error before the browser consent screen ever opens. ## Expected Behavior Witsy should register itself as a **public client** during DCR, since it is a desktop (Electron) app and cannot securely store a client secret. Per [RFC 8252 §8.4](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8252#section-8.4) and the [MCP authorization spec](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/draft/basic/authorization), native apps should use PKCE with `token_endpoint_auth_method: "none"`. The DCR registration POST body should look like: ```json { "client_name": "Witsy", "redirect_uris": ["http://127.0.0.1:/callback"], "grant_types": ["authorization_code", "refresh_token"], "response_types": ["code"], "token_endpoint_auth_method": "none", "scope": "" } ``` ## Actual Behavior Witsy either omits `token_endpoint_auth_method` (defaulting per RFC 7591 to `client_secret_basic`) or sends a non-`"none"` value, causing Fastmail's authorization server to reject the registration before the browser flow can start. ## Attempted Workarounds (None Worked) - Adding `token_endpoint_auth_method: none` as a custom HTTP header in the MCP server config. (This is not a header — it belongs in the DCR JSON body, so the workaround has no effect. Worth noting in case other users try the same thing.) - No UI option exists to override the DCR client metadata. ## Impact Multiple major remote MCP servers enforce public-client-only DCR and are currently unreachable from Witsy: | MCP Server | URL | Public-client DCR enforced | |---|---|---| | Fastmail | `https://api.fastmail.com/mcp` | ✅ | | Meta Ads | `https://mcp.facebook.com/ads` | ✅ (similar failure reported in [openai/codex#24103](https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/24103)) | | Linear | `https://mcp.linear.app/mcp` | Likely ✅ | | Notion | `https://mcp.notion.com/mcp` | Likely ✅ | ## Proposed Fix In Witsy's MCP OAuth client (the code handling `mcp-start-oauth-flow` in the Electron main process), set `token_endpoint_auth_method: "none"` in the DCR client metadata by default, since Witsy is inherently a public client. If a server returns `invalid_client_metadata` specifically referencing `token_endpoint_auth_method`, Witsy could optionally retry with `"none"` as a fallback, but making it the default is cleaner and spec-correct for a desktop app. Optional enhancements: - Allow users to supply a pre-registered `client_id` (and optional `client_secret`) per-server to skip DCR entirely — useful for servers that don't support DCR at all (e.g., GitHub Copilot MCP). See [FastMCP issue #3085](https://github.com/PrefectHQ/fastmcp/issues/3085) for prior art. - Surface the raw OAuth server error response in the UI / logs to make future debugging easier (the current truncated `nd:` prefix obscures it). ## Environment - **Witsy version:** - **OS:** - **MCP server tested:** `https://api.fastmail.com/mcp` (Fastmail, launched April 22, 2026) ## References - [RFC 7591 — OAuth 2.0 Dynamic Client Registration Protocol](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7591) - [RFC 8252 — OAuth 2.0 for Native Apps](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8252) - [MCP Authorization Specification](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/draft/basic/authorization) - Related issue in OpenAI Codex CLI for similar DCR failure: [openai/codex#24103](https://github.com/openai/codex/issues/24103) ## Additional Information OS: Windows 11 Pro for Workstations 25H2, Build 26200.8457 App Version: 3.5.2 Screenshot of the error message:

title: Windows native Alt+Win dictation shortcut can fire during other Alt combos
author: Qwinty
body:
## Summary
On Windows, a native multi-modifier quick dictation shortcut such as `Alt+Win` can trigger during unrelated `Alt+...` system shortcuts. In practice this can open quick dictation while using combinations such as `Alt+Tab` or `Alt+Shift`.

## Expected behavior
A native modifier-only combo should only activate when the exact configured modifier set is pressed, and should not fire if another key or an extra modifier joins the chord.

## Notes
The current key monitor activates multi-modifier combos as soon as the required modifiers are a subset of held modifiers. It also fires immediately on modifier down, before it can tell whether a regular key such as `Tab` is part of the same sequence.

title: Unable to save or return when editing AI Commands
author: Tom-Jerrr
body: **Describe the bug** When attempting to create a new AI Command or edit an existing one, the interface displays a critical usability flaw: 1. There is no visible "Save" button to commit the changes. 2. There is no "Back" or navigation control to return to the previous screen once inside the editing view. Users are effectively stuck in the editor mode if they do not know how to bypass it. To Reproduce Steps to reproduce the behavior: Navigate to the "AI Commands" section in the menu. Click on an existing command to open it for editing, or attempt to add a New Command. Attempt to look for a "Save" button to apply changes. Attempt to find a "Back" button or any way to close the window without crashing or getting stuck. Expected behavior There should be a clearly visible "Save" button to confirm the addition or modification of the command. Additionally, there should be a "Back" or "Cancel" button to allow the user to exit the editing interface without making changes. Screenshots I can attach screenshots showing the empty corners where the buttons should be located. Desktop (please complete the following information): OS: [Windows 10] Witsy Version 3.5.2 Additional context This seems to be an issue with the UI layout for the Command Editor component.

title: Support Microsoft login authentication flow to connect Foundry model without using the API key
author: anotheronebitesthedusttttz
body:
Visual Studio Code allows to connect to Azure Foundry models using Microsoft authentication rather than an API key.

The use case I have is that I got provisioned access to certain Foundry models, but the setup disallows to use API key, which disallows me to use Witsy with these models.

Do you have any plan to support such authentication?

title: Filesystem Enhancements - Alias for Directories
author: blu3knight
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
No

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Currently the directory structure that you can put in is treated as a whole directory, so when I instruct Witsy to go read a directory as part of the prompt it could be something like /Users/Username/Documents/watchamacult/thingamagicky/widgets which is a pain to type in and reference. 

It would be great if we can introduce aliases to this structure so the directory above could be aliased to "widgets" instead. 

The Logic besides it should check the alias to see if one exists and give an error if it does so that way an alias if exists could be renamed to "widget-1" and the new one can be called "widget-2" as an example. 

There should also be a way to remove the alias. 

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
1. Softlinks just translate to the actual location. 
2. APFS (Apple File System), used in modern macOS versions, does not support hard links for directories. 
3. Copying the Directories or Files which just use up more space. 


**Additional context**

title: Filesystem Enhancement - Permission per Directory
author: blu3knight
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
No

**Describe the solution you'd like**
In Witsy AI currently the Read/Write permissions are to all directories. As part of the work some directories should be with Read and some are with Write permissions same as (Unix/Windows). 

This would be simple by establishing a Read / Write buttons next to each directory and if the write button is not enabled not to ask for writes to that directory. 

While the system does prompt, a simple mistake with a yes answer can overwrite these files. 

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Using RAG just does not work very well for this, as it splits up these files (XML or Web Base) and with the large context sizes of local models now (Nemotron - 1M context), each file can be evaluated as needed. 

**Additional context**
None

title: Paste in the edit box inserts text in the end, not at the cursor position
author: symsymsym1
body:
**Describe the bug**
Since 3.5.x, when you insert a text from the clipboard, it gets inserted in the end of the whole text, not at the cursor position.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to any chat
2. Write/paste some text
3. Move cursor in the middle of the text
4. Try to paste additional texts from the clipboard.
5. It gets inserted in the end

**Expected behavior**
Text inserts where cursor stands

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - Windows 11
 - Witsy 3.5.2

**Additional context**
Also the editing (writing, etc) became slower, when you press buttons, texts sometimes appears in 0.5s or so - with noticeable delay. But I can't pinpoint the exact version when it happened.
